pattern: Track the three largest distinct values seen so far in a single pass, falling back to the maximum when fewer than three distinct values exist.
hints: Duplicates do not count as separate maxima, so you care only about distinct values.
You only ever need to remember the top three distinct values, not the whole array sorted.
Keep three running 'best' slots for the largest, second-largest and third-largest distinct numbers; if the third slot was never filled, return the largest instead.

edge_inputs.description: exactly three distinct values returns the smallest
only two distinct values, so the maximum is returned
duplicates collapse so the third distinct still exists
negative values including the minimum int, third distinct exists
all identical means one distinct value, return the maximum

stress.description: random ints with frequent duplicates so distinctness matters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    lo, hi = -50, 50
    return [[rng.randint(lo, hi) for _ in range(n)]]
stress.note: narrow value range forces many duplicates
